features.description: An action commits >=2 state-field changes atomically in one fired transition (kernel semantics: assignment=simultaneous).

evidence.detail: ok vectors whose `changes` touch >=2 state fields in one atomic step

features.description: An action's update right-hand side self-references a variable it also writes, proving updates read the pre-state (kernel semantics: reads=pre_state).

evidence.detail: action `add` updates a field using its own pre-update value and fired an ok vector
action `remove` updates a field using its own pre-update value and fired an ok vector
action `flip` updates a field using its own pre-update value and fired an ok vector

features.description: A false `requires` clause reports the action instance as not enabled instead of raising an error (kernel semantics: requires_false=not_enabled).
A violated action leaves the committed state unchanged, retaining only `attempted_state` for diagnostics (kernel semantics: failure_state=rollback).

evidence.detail: violation vectors that roll back to the unchanged committed state
violation vectors that roll back to the unchanged committed state

features.description: An `old(...)` expression is declared and reached by a real fired transition or the violation it causes (kernel semantics: old=pre_state).

evidence.detail: 4 `old(...)` expression(s) reached by a checked transition or the violation it causes
1 `old(...)` expression(s) reached by a checked transition or the violation it causes

features.description: A literal integer division with a negative operand fires a successful transition whose recorded value matches Euclidean division, not truncating division (kernel semantics: integer_division=euclidean).

The contract declares a `terminal` expression excluding matching states from deadlock reporting (kernel semantics: terminal_deadlock=terminal_states_excluded). Reaching a state with zero enabled actions at the declared terminal condition needs a BFS depth beyond the coverage corpus's chosen fixture depths, so only declared (not fired) evidence is required.

evidence.detail: contract declares a `terminal` expression excluding matching states from deadlock reporting

features.description: The contract marks at least one action `fair` (kernel semantics: fairness=weak). Weak fairness is a liveness property over infinite/untaken traces with no finite-vector witness, so only declared evidence is required.
A conformance vector with outcome.kind == "ok" is generated by the fixture corpus.
A conformance vector with outcome.kind == "requires_failed" is generated by the fixture corpus.
A conformance vector with outcome.kind == "partial_op" is generated by the fixture corpus.
A conformance vector with outcome.kind == "type_bound" is generated by the fixture corpus.
A conformance vector with outcome.kind == "invariant" is generated by the fixture corpus.
A conformance vector with outcome.kind == "trans" is generated by the fixture corpus.
A conformance vector with outcome.kind == "ensures" is generated by the fixture corpus.
A domain/range-typed state field's concrete value appears in a generated state snapshot.
A `Bool`-typed state field's concrete value appears in a generated state snapshot.
An enum-typed value appears in a generated state snapshot.
A struct-typed value appears in a generated state snapshot.

An `Option` value appears tagged `{"kind":"none"}`/`{"kind":"some","value":...}` in a generated state snapshot. A nested `Option<Option<_>>` tagged `some(none)` is additional, non-required evidence (see also the dedicated `conformance_distinguishes_nested_options_and_guard_partials` unit test, which is not part of the fixed fixture manifest this matrix scans).
A `Map` value appears in a generated state snapshot.
A `Set` value appears in a generated state snapshot.
A `Seq` value appears in a generated state snapshot.
The contract declares a `partial_operations` entry for `head` (a firing outcome.kind == "partial_op" vector unambiguously attributable to it is additional, non-required evidence).
The contract declares a `partial_operations` entry for `pop` (a firing outcome.kind == "partial_op" vector unambiguously attributable to it is additional, non-required evidence).
The contract declares a `partial_operations` entry for `at` (a firing outcome.kind == "partial_op" vector unambiguously attributable to it is additional, non-required evidence).
The contract declares a `partial_operations` entry for `index` (a firing outcome.kind == "partial_op" vector unambiguously attributable to it is additional, non-required evidence).
The contract declares a `partial_operations` entry for `divide` (a firing outcome.kind == "partial_op" vector unambiguously attributable to it is additional, non-required evidence).
The contract declares a `partial_operations` entry for `remainder` (a firing outcome.kind == "partial_op" vector unambiguously attributable to it is additional, non-required evidence).
A `forall`/`exists` quantified expression is declared and evaluated across a real fired transition.

evidence.detail: 1 forall/exists expression(s) evaluated across 6 ok vector(s)

features.description: A typed/range action parameter's finite domain is declared and enumerated across >=2 distinct fired parameter bindings.

evidence.detail: action `add` fired 2 distinct typed/range parameter bindings

features.description: An action retains its requirement ID in the contract and fires at least one conformance vector.

evidence.detail: action `add` retains requirement id `REQ-208-ADD` and fired 14 vector(s)
action `remove` retains requirement id `REQ-208-REMOVE` and fired 7 vector(s)
